commitMessage: Smooth Iron Snake progression; add head/tail graphics; fix death overlay
summary: The developer implemented smooth Iron Snake progression (keeping snake length/shape across levels with proportional board growth), plus several visual improvements: a prominent snake head with direction-facing eyes, a tapered tail, depth-correct death block rendering, and a repositioned death banner.

steps.description: Board area scales with level: new exported helper ironSnakeGridSizeForArea in iron-snake.ts, new method ironSnakeGridSizeForLevel in main.ts, new const IRON_SNAKE_GOAL_FILL = 0.4
steps.note: All three additions are present in the diff: `ironSnakeGridSizeForArea` exported from iron-snake.ts using `TARGET_FILL_MID` (the midpoint of LO/HI band), `IRON_SNAKE_GOAL_FILL = 0.4` constant in main.ts, and `ironSnakeGridSizeForLevel` method. The 40% fill target was chosen as 'tighter/harder' — the board feels roomy at level start and tightens as the snake grows toward the goal, with the floor ensuring level-1 behavior is byte-identical to before.

steps.description: Grow the board using the existing gridSize-change plumbing: set new gridSize before regenerating mask, then updateCanvasSize
steps.note: The reworked `advanceLevel` sets `this.gridSize = baseGrid + extra` before calling `regenerateIronSnakeBoard()`, then calls `updateCanvasSize()` at the end — reusing the exact same plumbing as classic `expandGrid`. The retry loop (extra 0..3) was added to give the shape generator more room when a generated shape can't seat the snakes.

steps.description: Preserve the snake's shape on advance: capture relative offsets, placeRigidSnake helper with identity-first then rotation orientations, fallback chain
steps.note: The full fallback chain is implemented: `placeRigid` (rigid placement with 4 orientations), `layoutBody` (Warnsdorff-guided self-avoiding walk — chosen over greedy straight-first after the greedy approach painted itself into corners on irregular boards with long snakes), `reseatKeepingShape` orchestrating both, and the rectangle/length-1 backstops. The Warnsdorff heuristic was a key decision: it reliably seats long snakes without expensive backtracking. `sortedCandidates` was factored out of `findStartCell` to be shared with `placeRigid` as planned.

steps.description: Wire the new gridSize into level start and advance: advanceLevel uses ironSnakeGridSizeForLevel, startNewGame and +/- handler use ironSnakeGridSizeForLevel(1)
steps.note: Both `startNewGame` (line ~1227) and the `+/-` handler (line ~982) now call `this.ironSnakeGridSizeForLevel(1)` instead of `Math.max(this.initialGridSize, IRON_SNAKE_MIN_SIZE)`, and `advanceLevel` uses `ironSnakeGridSizeForLevel(this.level)` as the base grid size before the retry loop.

steps.description: Cleanups: fix maxLength1/2 no-op ordering, update stale comment about board staying same size
steps.note: The stale comment 'since the board stays the same size each level' is updated to 'the board grows with the level'. The `maxLength1/2` lines are kept in place — with shape preservation the snake retains its real length, so they now correctly capture the pre-advance max rather than being no-ops as they were when length was reset to 1 before them.
